# =============================================================================

# PLUGS FRAMEWORK — ENVIRONMENT CONFIGURATION

# =============================================================================

#

# This file defines all configurable environment variables for the Plugs

# framework. Copy this file to `.env` in your project root and fill in

# the values for your specific environment.

#

# cp .env.example .env

#

# IMPORTANT: Never commit your `.env` file to version control. It contains

# sensitive credentials (API keys, database passwords, encryption keys).

# The `.env.example` file IS safe to commit — it serves as documentation.

#

# Lines starting with # are comments. Variables that are commented out with

# a # prefix are optional and show their default values for reference.

#

# =============================================================================

# =============================================================================

# APPLICATION SETTINGS

# =============================================================================

# Core settings that define how your application behaves globally.

# The display name of your application.

# Used in page titles, emails, SEO meta tags, and the admin panel.

APP_NAME="My Plugs App"

# The current application environment.

# Options: local, staging, production

# WHY: Controls debug output, error verbosity, asset minification, and

# security behaviors. ALWAYS set to "production" on live servers.

APP_ENV=local

# Enable or disable detailed error pages and debug info.

# Options: true, false

# WHY: When true, full stack traces and query logs are shown on errors.

# NEVER enable in production — it leaks sensitive internal details.

APP_DEBUG=true

# The public URL where your application is accessible.

# WHY: Used to generate absolute URLs for links, assets, redirects, and

# OAuth callbacks. Must match your actual domain in production.

APP_URL=http://plugs.local

# The default timezone for your application.

# WHY: Affects date/time functions, logging timestamps, scheduled tasks,

# and database datetime handling. Use a valid PHP timezone identifier.

# Examples: UTC, America/New_York, Europe/London, Africa/Lagos, Asia/Tokyo

APP_TIMEZONE=Africa/Lagos

# The application encryption key (base64 encoded, 32 bytes).

# WHY: Used by the Encrypter (AES-256-GCM) to encrypt cookies, sessions,

# and any data you encrypt via encrypt(). Without this, your app

# cannot securely encrypt or decrypt data.

# Generate with: php plugs key:generate

APP_KEY=

# The default locale for the application.

# WHY: Determines which language/translation files are loaded first.

#APP_LOCALE=en

# Fallback locale when a translation string is missing in the active locale.

#APP_FALLBACK_LOCALE=en

# The active UI theme for the application.

# WHY: Determines which theme directory is used under resources/views/themes/.

#APP_THEME=default

# =============================================================================

# CACHE & SESSION CONFIGURATION

# =============================================================================

# Controls how temporary data and user sessions are stored.

# The cache storage backend.

# Options: file, redis

# WHY: "file" is simplest and works everywhere. "redis" is faster and

# recommended for production when multiple app servers share state.

CACHE_DRIVER=file

# The session storage backend.

# Options: file, database

# WHY: "database" is more reliable for multi-server deployments and makes

# sessions queryable (e.g., to see active users). "file" is fine for

# single-server setups.

SESSION_DRIVER=database

# Session lifetime in minutes before it expires due to inactivity.

# WHY: Shorter = more secure (auto-logout). Longer = better UX.

# 120 minutes (2 hours) is a reasonable balance.

SESSION_LIFETIME=120

# =============================================================================

# LOGGING CONFIGURATION

# =============================================================================

# Controls how application logs are written and stored.

# The logging channel to use.

# Options: file (single log file), daily (rotated daily, keeps 14 days)

# WHY: "daily" is recommended for production to prevent log files from

# growing indefinitely and consuming all disk space.

LOG_CHANNEL=file

# Minimum log level to record.

# Options (ascending severity): debug, info, notice, warning, error,

# critical, alert, emergency

# WHY: Use "debug" in development to see everything. Use "warning" or

# "error" in production to reduce noise and disk usage.

LOG_LEVEL=debug

# =============================================================================

# FILESYSTEM CONFIGURATION

# =============================================================================

# Defines which storage disk is used by default for file operations.

# The default filesystem disk.

# Options: local (private storage), public (publicly accessible via URL), s3

# WHY: "public" makes uploaded files (images, documents) accessible via the

# web. "local" keeps files private. "s3" offloads to Amazon S3 / compatible.

FILESYSTEM_DISK=public

# --- Amazon S3 / Compatible Storage (Optional) ---

# Required only if FILESYSTEM_DISK=s3 or if you use the 's3' disk explicitly.

#AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID=
#AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY=
#AWS_DEFAULT_REGION=us-east-1
#AWS_BUCKET=
#AWS_URL=
#AWS_ENDPOINT=
#AWS_USE_PATH_STYLE_ENDPOINT=false
#AWS_ROOT=

# =============================================================================

# QUEUE CONFIGURATION

# =============================================================================

# Controls how background jobs (emails, notifications, heavy tasks) are processed.

# The default queue connection.

# Options: sync, database, redis

# WHY: "sync" processes jobs immediately (blocking). Good for development.

# "database" or "redis" process jobs in the background via a worker,

# which is essential for production performance (non-blocking).

QUEUE_CONNECTION=sync

# The database table name for storing queued jobs (when using "database" driver).

QUEUE_TABLE=jobs

# The database table for failed jobs (for retry/debugging).

#FAILED_JOB_TABLE=failed_jobs

# --- Redis Connection (Optional) ---

# Required if CACHE_DRIVER=redis or QUEUE_CONNECTION=redis.

# WHY: Redis is an in-memory data store that provides much faster caching

# and queue processing compared to file/database backends.

REDIS*HOST=127.0.0.1
REDIS_PORT=6379
REDIS_PASSWORD=null
REDIS_PREFIX=plugs_database*
#REDIS_QUEUE=default

# =============================================================================

# SECURITY CONFIGURATION

# =============================================================================

# These settings control the framework's multi-layered security system.

# Enable CSRF (Cross-Site Request Forgery) protection.

# Options: true, false

# WHY: Prevents attackers from tricking authenticated users into submitting

# malicious requests. Should ALWAYS be true in production.

CSRF_ENABLED=true

# Enable CSRF Strict Mode for enhanced token validation.

# Options: true, false

# WHY: Enforces stricter token lifetime checks, per-request token consumption,

# and BREACH-resistant token masking. Provides best-in-class CSRF defense.

# Disable only if you have a specific compatibility reason.

CSRF_STRICT_MODE=true

# Enable Content Security Policy (CSP) headers.

# Options: true, false

# WHY: CSP tells browsers which sources of content (scripts, styles, images)

# are trusted. Prevents XSS attacks by blocking inline scripts and

# unauthorized external resources. Enable in production after testing.

CSP_ENABLED=false

# Enable Cross-Origin Resource Sharing (CORS).

# Options: true, false

# WHY: Required if your frontend (e.g., React/Vue SPA) is on a different

# domain than your API. Without CORS enabled, browsers will block

# cross-origin API requests. Keep false if frontend and backend share

# the same domain.

CORS_ENABLED=false

# Enable the Security Shield middleware.

# Options: true, false

# WHY: Provides real-time threat detection including rate limiting (brute

# force/DDoS protection), bot detection (blocks scrapers/crawlers),

# and behavioral analysis. Should be true in production.

SECURITY_SHIELD=true

# Enable the request/performance profiler toolbar.

# Options: true, false

# WHY: Shows a debug toolbar with query counts, execution time, memory

# usage, and route info. Useful for development. MUST be false in

# production — it exposes internal application details.

PROFILER_ENABLED=false

# Enable the Identity Manager for device/session tracking.

# Options: true, false

# WHY: Tracks user identities across sessions for features like "remember

# this device" and suspicious login detection.

AUTH_IDENTITY_ENABLED=true

# Require email verification after user registration.

# Options: true, false

# WHY: When true, newly registered users must verify their email before

# gaining full access. Prevents fake/spam accounts.

AUTH_VERIFICATION=false

# Commma-separated list of trusted proxy IP addresses or CIDR ranges.

# REQUIRED if your app is behind a load balancer or reverse proxy (Nginx,

# Cloudflare, AWS ALB), you need to trust the proxy so the framework reads

# the correct client IP from X-Forwarded-For or CF-Connecting-IP headers.

# WHY: Prevents identifying your own load balancer as the "attacker" and

# ensures rate limits and logs use the correct client IP address.

# Examples: 10.0.0.1, 172.64.0.0/16 or "\*" to trust all (not recommended).

#TRUSTED_PROXIES=

# =============================================================================

# DATABASE CONFIGURATION

# =============================================================================

# Connection details for your primary database.

# The database driver to use.

# Options: mysql, pgsql, sqlite

# WHY: Determines which database engine the framework connects to.

DB_CONNECTION=mysql

# The database server hostname or IP address.

DB_HOST=127.0.0.1

# The database server port.

# Defaults: MySQL=3306, PostgreSQL=5432

DB_PORT=3306

# The name of the database to connect to.

DB_DATABASE=plugs

# Database authentication credentials.

# WHY: These are the username and password used to authenticate with

# the database server. Use a dedicated user with minimal privileges

# in production — never use root.

DB_USERNAME=root
DB_PASSWORD=

# Read replica host (Optional, for read/write splitting).

# WHY: In high-traffic production setups, you can route SELECT queries

# to read replicas to reduce load on the primary database server.

# If not set, all queries go to DB_HOST.

#DB_READ_HOST=

# Enable database connection pooling.

# Options: true, false

# WHY: Reuses existing database connections instead of creating new ones

# for every request. Reduces connection overhead in high-traffic apps.

#DB_POOL_ENABLED=false

# =============================================================================

# MAIL CONFIGURATION

# =============================================================================

# Settings for sending transactional emails (verification, password reset, etc).

# The mail transport driver.

# Options: smtp, sendmail, mailgun, ses

MAIL_MAILER=smtp

# SMTP server hostname.

# Examples: smtp.mailtrap.io (testing), smtp.gmail.com, smtp.sendgrid.net

MAIL_HOST=smtp.mailtrap.io

# SMTP server port.

# Common ports: 25 (unencrypted), 465 (SSL), 587 (TLS — recommended)

MAIL_PORT=2525

# SMTP authentication credentials.

# WHY: Most SMTP servers require authentication. Get these from your

# email provider's dashboard (Mailtrap, SendGrid, Mailgun, etc).

MAIL_USERNAME=
MAIL_PASSWORD=

# Encryption protocol for the SMTP connection.

# Options: tls (recommended), ssl, null (no encryption — insecure)

# WHY: TLS encrypts the connection between your server and the mail

# server, preventing credentials and email content from being

# intercepted in transit.

MAIL_ENCRYPTION=tls

# The "From" address and name used in outgoing emails.

# WHY: Recipients see this as the sender. Use a real address that

# matches your domain's SPF/DKIM records to avoid spam filters.

MAIL_FROM_ADDRESS=noreply@example.com
MAIL_FROM_NAME="${APP_NAME}"

# =============================================================================

# HASHING CONFIGURATION

# =============================================================================

# Controls how passwords and sensitive data are hashed.

# The hashing algorithm driver.

# Options: bcrypt, argon2i, argon2id

# WHY: argon2id is the most secure option — it's resistant to both GPU

# and side-channel attacks. bcrypt is a solid fallback if your PHP

# build doesn't support Argon2. NEVER use MD5 or SHA for passwords.

HASH_DRIVER=argon2id

# --- Bcrypt Options ---

# Number of hashing rounds (cost factor). Higher = slower but more secure.

# Default: 12. Recommended range: 10-14.

#BCRYPT_ROUNDS=12

# --- Argon2id Options ---

# Memory usage in KiB (default: 65536 = 64MB).

# Higher = more resistant to GPU attacks but uses more server RAM.

#ARGON2ID_MEMORY=65536

# Time cost (number of iterations). Higher = slower but more secure.

#ARGON2ID_TIME=4

# Number of parallel threads. Match to your server's CPU core count.

#ARGON2ID_THREADS=3

# Automatically rehash passwords on login when the algorithm/cost changes.

# Options: true, false

# WHY: If you upgrade from bcrypt to argon2id, enabling this will

# transparently re-hash user passwords the next time they log in,

# so all passwords migrate to the stronger algorithm over time.

#HASH_AUTO_REHASH=false

# =============================================================================

# VIEW / TEMPLATE ENGINE CONFIGURATION

# =============================================================================

# Controls the template rendering engine.

# The view engine driver.

# Options: plug (default Plugs engine), blade, twig

#VIEW_DRIVER=plug

# Enable response streaming (sends HTML to the browser as it's rendered).

# Options: true, false

# WHY: Improves perceived page load speed — users see content faster

# because the browser can start rendering before PHP finishes.

#VIEW_STREAMING=false

# Number of output rows before auto-flushing the buffer during streaming.

#VIEW_AUTO_FLUSH=50

# Custom path to the views directory (if different from default).

#VIEW_PATH=

# =============================================================================

# OPCACHE CONFIGURATION

# =============================================================================

# PHP OPcache settings for production performance optimization.

# Enable OPcache integration.

# Options: true, false

# WHY: OPcache compiles PHP scripts to bytecode and caches them in memory,

# dramatically improving performance (30-100% faster). Essential for

# production. Disable only for debugging opcode issues.

#OPCACHE_ENABLED=true

# Validate file timestamps to detect changes.

# Options: true, false

# WHY: Set to false in production (files don't change between deploys).

# Set to true in development so code changes are picked up immediately.

#OPCACHE_VALIDATE_TIMESTAMPS=false

# How often (in seconds) to check for file changes (only if validate_timestamps=true).

#OPCACHE_REVALIDATE_FREQ=0

# Maximum number of PHP files that can be cached.

#OPCACHE_MAX_ACCELERATED_FILES=10000

# Memory allocated to OPcache in megabytes.

#OPCACHE_MEMORY_CONSUMPTION=128

# Memory for interned strings in megabytes.

#OPCACHE_INTERNED_STRINGS_BUFFER=8

# Use fast shutdown sequence to free memory faster.

#OPCACHE_FAST_SHUTDOWN=true

# =============================================================================

# PAYMENT GATEWAY CONFIGURATION (Optional)

# =============================================================================

# Configure payment processors for your application's billing features.

# Default Payment Platform

# Options: paystack, flutterwave, stripe, paypal, payoneer, btcpay

# WHY: Determines which payment gateway is used when processing transactions

# without explicitly specifying a provider.

DEFAULT_PAYMENT_PLATFORM=paystack

# Payment Environment

# Options: test, live

# WHY: "test" uses sandbox/mock transactions so no real money is charged.

# Switch to "live" only when you're ready to accept real payments.

PAYMENT_ENVIRONMENT=test

# Default Currency

# Examples: NGN, USD, GHS, KES, ZAR, EUR, GBP

# WHY: The currency used for transactions when not specified per-request.

PAYMENT_CURRENCY=NGN

# --- Paystack (https://dashboard.paystack.com/#/settings/developer) ---

PAYSTACK_PUBLIC_KEY=pk_test_your_public_key_here
PAYSTACK_SECRET_KEY=sk_test_your_secret_key_here
PAYSTACK_WEBHOOK_SECRET=your_webhook_secret_here

# --- Flutterwave (https://dashboard.flutterwave.com/settings/apis) ---

FLUTTERWAVE_PUBLIC_KEY=FLWPUBK_TEST-your_public_key_here
FLUTTERWAVE_SECRET_KEY=FLWSECK_TEST-your_secret_key_here
FLUTTERWAVE_ENCRYPTION_KEY=FLWSECK_TEST-your_encryption_key_here

# --- Stripe (https://dashboard.stripe.com/apikeys) ---

#STRIPE_PUBLIC_KEY=pk_test_your_stripe_public_key
#STRIPE_SECRET_KEY=sk_test_your_stripe_secret_key
#STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET=whsec_your_webhook_secret

# --- PayPal (https://developer.paypal.com/) ---

#PAYPAL_MODE=sandbox
#PAYPAL_CLIENT_ID=your_paypal_client_id
#PAYPAL_CLIENT_SECRET=your_paypal_client_secret

# --- Payoneer (https://developer.payoneer.com/) ---

#PAYONEER_MODE=sandbox
#PAYONEER_CLIENT_ID=your_payoneer_client_id
#PAYONEER_CLIENT_SECRET=your_payoneer_client_secret
#PAYONEER_PROGRAM_ID=your_payoneer_program_id

# =============================================================================

# OAUTH / SOCIAL LOGIN CONFIGURATION (Optional)

# =============================================================================

# Enable "Login with Google/GitHub/etc." functionality.

# Get credentials from each provider's developer console.

# --- Google OAuth (https://console.cloud.google.com/apis/credentials) ---

# WHY: Allows users to sign in with their Google account.

GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID=
GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET=
#GOOGLE_REDIRECT_URI=http://plugs.local/auth/google/callback

# --- Facebook OAuth (https://developers.facebook.com/apps/) ---

FACEBOOK_CLIENT_ID=
FACEBOOK_CLIENT_SECRET=

# --- GitHub OAuth (https://github.com/settings/developers) ---

# WHY: Popular for developer-facing applications.

GITHUB_CLIENT_ID=
GITHUB_CLIENT_SECRET=
#GITHUB_REDIRECT_URI=http://plugs.local/auth/github/callback

# --- Discord OAuth (https://discord.com/developers/applications) ---

DISCORD_CLIENT_ID=
DISCORD_CLIENT_SECRET=

# =============================================================================

# AI PROVIDER CONFIGURATION (Optional)

# =============================================================================

# Configure AI providers for features like content moderation, auto-generation,

# and the Self-Healing CLI. Only configure the provider(s) you plan to use.

# The default AI provider to use.

# Options: openai, anthropic, gemini, groq, openrouter

# WHY: Determines which AI service handles requests from the AI facade

# when no specific provider is specified per-call.

#AI_DRIVER=openai

# --- OpenAI (https://platform.openai.com/api-keys) ---

#OPENAI_API_KEY=
#OPENAI_ORGANIZATION=
#OPENAI_MODEL=gpt-4o

# --- Anthropic / Claude (https://console.anthropic.com/) ---

#ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=
#ANTHROPIC_MODEL=claude-opus-4-6

# --- Google Gemini (https://aistudio.google.com/apikey) ---

#GEMINI_API_KEY=
#GEMINI_MODEL=gemini-3-flash-preview

# --- Groq (https://console.groq.com/keys) ---

#GROQ_API_KEY=
#GROQ_MODEL=llama-3.3-70b-versatile

# --- OpenRouter (https://openrouter.ai/keys) ---

#OPENROUTER_API_KEY=
#OPENROUTER_MODEL=openrouter/auto:free
